intro.title: Some rooms don't just change what gets built. They *change who builds it together.*
intro.body:
Some rooms change what gets built.

Usually, it's just a conversation, an opposing idea, perhaps a difficult question that ignites something. 

*A room full of people who care enough to build a better world.*

Ummah Tech exists to create those rooms. Not to tell you what to think, but to bring together people who are willing to think deeply, build responsibly and shape technology with faith, purpose and responsibility.

Because great civilizations were never built in isolation.

milestones.title: The first conference
milestones.body:
Ummah Tech launched its inaugural conference in Johannesburg in November 2024, bringing together Muslim founders, technologists, creatives and community leaders to explore what it means to build technology with faith, purpose and impact at its centre.
The conversations sparked new collaborations, challenged assumptions and laid the foundations for a growing community of builders committed to using technology in service of the Ummah and humanity. Faith × Technology × Community

image.alt: A panel discussion at the first Ummah Tech Conference

milestones.title: Level 1 | Building Madinah
milestones.body:
Every great civilization begins with foundations.

In 2025, we looked to Madinah, not as a blueprint to replicate, but as a framework for thinking about the future. Drawing on the enduring principles and systems established during the time of the Prophet ﷺ, Building Madinah explored how faith can inform the technologies, institutions and communities we build today.

Together, founders, technologists, scholars and leaders asked not only what can we build?, but what is worth building?

That conversation continues with Level 2: Growing Madinah.

milestones.title: Coming up: Level 2 | Growing Madinah
milestones.body:
The Ummah is building.

Now comes the work of growing.

Level 2: Growing Madinah explores how we move beyond individual projects to nurture the institutions, communities and systems needed for a flourishing civilisation.

Join founders, technologists, creatives, researchers and leaders asking not only what is worth building, but how we build it to endure.

Welcome to Level 2: Growing Madinah.

image.alt: The Ummah Tech community gathered in Cape Town

items.title: Big Tech displaces public institutions
items.body: Private companies are taking on roles once held by public institutions, the values embedded in those systems matter more than ever.

items.title: Algorithms shape belief and behaviour
items.body: Recommendation engines don't just organise information, they shape attention, culture and ultimately society. We need builders who understand both the technology and responsibility.

items.title: Our voices are censored
items.body: Our ability to communicate, organise and share ideas depends on platforms we do not own or govern. For example, posts about the genocide in Gaza are being shadow banned on social media platforms. Communities that don't build their own infrastructure do not have a platform to share the truth.

items.title: Dependent on providers we boycott
items.body: Many of the technologies we rely on every day are built by organisations whose values do not reflect our own. Google, Amazon and Microsoft sell cloud and AI services to the Israeli military.  Even when we want to boycott these companies or platforms, meaningful alternatives often don't exist.

gallery.heading: Find your people
gallery.body: If you are a Muslim technologist, founder, creative or innovator, you belong here. Because if a future worth inheriting is being built, the people building it should know each other.

Attendees praying together at the conference
A talk in progress in the main room

beliefs.heading: What we stand for
beliefs.lead: These propositions shape what we build and how we build it.

items.title: _Niyyah_ (sincere intention).
items.body: Every action is driven by a pure, purposeful intention to benefit the Ummah and humanity.

items.title: Technological independence.
items.body: Striving for independence in technology to secure the Ummah's future.

items.title: Values-based technology design.
items.body: Technology is not neutral. We design and deploy it consciously, embedding Islamic values of justice, fairness and integrity in its foundations.

items.title: Rethinking progress.
items.body: Progress measured beyond GDP and consumption: justice, poverty reduction, morality and flourishing communities.

items.title: Creativity and innovation.
items.body: We encourage futuristic thinking, keeping Muslim technologists at the forefront of global tech.

contact.heading: Get in touch.
contact.description: Questions, speaking or partnership. Tell us what you're reaching out about and it reaches the right person.
contact.successMessage: Thanks, we've got your message. We'll get back to you soon.
